etymology.description: semantic 士(士) (shì, "scholar") + phonetic 寸(寸) (cùn) -> longevity
etymology.interpretation: 壽(壽) (shòu, "longevity") consists of 士(士) (shì, "scholar"), representing an esteemed, noble quality, combined with the phonetic 寸(寸) (cùn), indicating the measurement of life. Together they convey the notion of a long, honored life.
etymology.simplification: Simplified 寿 from traditional 壽 involves the omission of 示(示) (shì, "sign") and retaining the common phonetic component, 寸(寸) (cùn), focusing on the pronunciation without the semantic complexity.

examples.chinese: 爷爷的寿命(爺爺的壽命)很长(很長)，大家都希望他健康长寿(大家都希望他健康長壽)。
examples.pinyin: Yéye de shòumìng hěn cháng, dàjiā dōu xīwàng tā jiànkāng chángshòu.
examples.english: Grandpa's life is very long; everyone hopes he stays healthy and has a long life.

examples.chinese: 在传统文化中(在傳統文化中)，龟象征着长寿(龜象徵著長壽)。
examples.pinyin: Zài chuántǒng wénhuà zhōng, guī xiàngzhēngzhe chángshòu.
examples.english: In traditional culture, the turtle symbolizes longevity.

examples.chinese: 他们为奶奶的八十大寿(他們為奶奶的八十大壽)举行了一个盛大的庆祝。(舉行了一個盛大的慶祝)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen wèi nǎinai de bāshí dàshòu jǔxíngle yīgè shèngdà de qìngzhù.
examples.english: They held a grand celebration for grandma's 80th birthday.
